title: -Contract: B Bros Construction, Inc. for Fire Station #6 Remodel

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for Fire Station #6 Remodel (Project),
Specification No. 26-11760-C; and
2. Accepting the bid of B Bros Construction, Inc. as the lowest, responsive and
responsible bidder; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions, or change orders until the Project is completed in accordance with
the approved plans and specifications for an amount not to exceed $597,913.20,
which includes a contingency of $99,652.20.
sections.fiscal_impacts:

sections.current_situation:
Fire Station 6 is located at 999 Cedar Street. The station consists of two apparatus
rooms, three sleeping rooms, a kitchen, and an office. The station was rebuilt in 1999.
Currently, the building contains several deficient windows. Deficiencies include peeling
paint around windowsills, inoperable windows, and deteriorating framing. This Project
will replace 14 windows at the station, add an ADA-compliant lift to the front of the
station, and other ancillary related work.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Contract: B Bros Construction, Inc. for Fire Station #6 Remodel CONSENT CALENDAR
January 27, 2026
The Project was advertised for bids on November 6, 2025, and bids were opened on
December 4, 2025. The City received 12 bids for the project ranging from $348,800 to
$685,000. The lowest responsive and responsible bidder was determined to be B Bros
Construction, Inc. Staff recommends that a construction contract for the Project be
awarded to B Bros Construction, Inc.
The Fire Station #6 Remodel Project is in alignment with the City’s Strategic Plan
Priorities, advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
Fire Station 6 was rebuilt in 1999. It is comprised of apparatus storage and living
quarters for fire department staff. The renovation project is a Measure T1 Phase 2
project.
The City engaged the services of an architectural consultant to assess the condition of
various city buildings in 2021. After building deficiencies were cataloged, the City
engaged an architectural consultant in July 2022 to help identify the scope for
improvements and begin design of the Project. In October 2025, permit documents
were approved and the project was advertised for public bid on November 6, 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This project will address the City’s sustainability and climate goals by increasing energy
efficiency, and by providing newer, easily operable windows to the station.
sections.rationale:
Contracted services are required for this project as the City does not have the in-house
expertise to complete this specialized work.
sections.alternatives:
The City can elect not to construct the project; however, deferring the work will lead to
deterioration of the existing windows and continued discomfort for the fire department
personnel stationed there. Postponing the project will escalate costs and cost the City
more money in the future as deterioration continues.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Department of Public Works (510) 981-6396
Priya Chopra; Associate Civil Engineer, Department of Public Works (510) 981-6338
sections.attachments:
1. Resolution
2. Bid Abstract
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: B BROS CONSTRUCTION, INC, FOR FIRE STATION #6 REMODEL;
SPECIFICATION NO. 26-11760-C, AT 999 CEDAR STREET
WHEREAS, the City engaged the services of an architectural consultant to assess the
condition of various City buildings in 2021; and
WHEREAS, after building deficiencies were cataloged in Fire Station 6, the City engaged
an architectural consultant in July 2022, to help identify the scope for improvements and
begin design of the Project; and
WHEREAS, the project consists of window replacement and ADA upgrades at Fire
Station 6; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
project; and
WHEREAS; in October 2025, permit documents were approved and the Project was
advertised for bids on November 6, 2025 (Plans and Specifications No. 26-11760-C) and
opened on December 4, 2025; and
WHEREAS, B Bros Construction Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive and
responsible bidder.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specifications No. 26-11760-C for Fire Station #6 Remodel are approved.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley accepts the bid of
B Bros Construction as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes the City
Manager to execute a contract and any amendments, extensions, or change orders, until
completion of the project in accordance with the approved plans and specifications with
B Bros Construction, Inc for the Fire Station #6 Remodel in an amount not to exceed
$597,913.20, which includes a contingency in the amount of $99,652.20. A record
signature copy of the agreement and any amendments will be on file in the Office of the
City Clerk.
Page 4 of 4
